item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit that the containment procedures being struck out from Safe to Euclid, and the description of the -A's activities, gave away some of the punchline about what happened to the doctor.

But it came back strong with the crows impregnating females with barley seeds. The imagery there is very squick, but not so much to churn my stomach.

Nicely written! Thanks for the entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly think it’s a little too straightforward in its current state, but I still +1’d it for being a great idea. It could use a litle more mystery, but it’s put together very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the discordant contrast between the folksy midwestern diction and the horrific actions of the scarecrows. The death of the doctor was easily predicted, but the phenomena that occurred afterwards definitely caught me pleasantly off guard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On reflection, I think I like this one. Like, the scarecrow people kind of took me out of it the first time, but between the atmosphere and just, like, the ability to teleport through barley. I can't get enough of that. This is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first attempt at a good old-fashioned horror skip. Many thanks to Countless Skies for providing critique.

Image was created by Rick Harris and is borrowed from here.

To see more of my work, check out my Author Page!
